raw.objective: “to determine the rate and extent of CBZ biotransformation to EPO and to develop a pharmacokinetic model of the fate of CBZ and EPO following CBZ administration in humans” (EPO=carbamazepine epoxide)
raw.covariates: WT on Vmax, WT on V2, WT on V4
raw.observations: A very ambitious model considering the year of publication. Allows predicting CBZ to CBZE conversion including different types of absorption, as well as infusion. Covariate effects are taken implicit in the form of parameters being *kg^-1. The small range in age and weight does limit the applicability of the model regarding other populations. Unexplained variance is not described, which does not allow for predicting a range of possible concentrations in the population
raw.dose: Multiple doses through multiple routes in healthy subjects
raw.structure: 2 compartment linear absorption and combined linear and nonlinear elimination
raw.equations: KaS=theta1 KaA=theta2 KaB=theta3 KaC=theta4 K23=theta5 K32= theta6 K45= theta7 K54= theta8 Vmax= theta9*WT Km= theta10 Ke2= theta11 Ke4= theta12 V2= theta13*WT V4= theta14*WT C2=A2/V2 dA1/dt=DOSE – KA #different KA depending on tablet versions dA2/dt= KA –K23*A2 +K32*A3 –Ke2*A2 –(Vmax*C2)/(Km+C2) dA3/dt= K23*A2 – K32*A3 –Ke4*A4

structural_model.description: 2 compartment linear absorption and combined linear and nonlinear elimination

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Multiple doses through multiple routes in healthy subjects
study.objective: “to determine the rate and extent of CBZ biotransformation to EPO and to develop a pharmacokinetic model of the fate of CBZ and EPO following CBZ administration in humans” (EPO=carbamazepine epoxide)

covariates.raw: WT on Vmax, WT on V2, WT on V4

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
